title: oxytheca parishii var. abramsii
intro: Oxytheca parishii var. abramsii, also known as Abrams' oxytheca, is a rare and endemic species of flowering plant in the daisy family, specifically found in the coastal sage scrub of California. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Abrams' oxytheca include treating skin conditions, wound healing, and reducing inflammation. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or infused oil for its medicinal uses, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and optimal preparation methods.
